session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 35 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 113 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

88   MIGRATION_STATE_NONE,     
89   MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, 
90   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
91   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
92 } migration_state_t;

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2421 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2421                                                                               {
2422   if (!session) {
2423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2424     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2425   }
2426 
2427   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2428            session->participant_id[1]);
2429 
2430   // Get configured port (or use default)
2431   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2432 
2433   // Mark ourselves as host
2434   session->is_host = true;
2435 
2436   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2437   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2438     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2439         .port = host_port,
2440         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2441         .max_clients = 32,
2442         .encryption_enabled = true,
2443     };
2444 
2445     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2446     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2447       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2448       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2449     }
2450 
2451     // Start listening for new participant connections
2452     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2453     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2454       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2455       return hstart;
2456     }
2457 
2458     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2459   }
2460 
2461   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2462   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2463   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2464   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2465   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2466   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2467   // Set connection type based on NAT quality (for now use DIRECT_PUBLIC for localhost dev environments)
2468   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2469 
2470   if (session->acds_transport) {
2471     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2472                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2473     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2474              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2475   }
2476 
2477   // Mark migration complete
2478   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2479 
2480   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2481   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2482 
2483   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2484 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2298 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2298                                                                                    {
2299   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2300     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2301     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2302   }
2303 
2304   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2305   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2306     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2307     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2308       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2309     }
2310     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2311     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2312   }
2313 
2314   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2315   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2316   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2317     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2318   }
2319 
2320   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2321   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2322     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2323   }
2324 
2325   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2326 
2327   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2328   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2329     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2330     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2331       // Ping timed out
2332       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2333       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2334       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2335                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2336 
2337       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2338       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2339         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2340         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2341       }
2342     }
2343   }
2344 
2345   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2346   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2347   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2348     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2349     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2350     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2351       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2352       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2353         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2354         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2355         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2356                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2357       } else {
2358         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2359         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2360         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2361           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2362           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2363         }
2364       }
2365     }
2366   }
2367 
2368   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2369 }

Parameters

2486                                                                                          {
2487   if (!session) {
2488     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2489     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2490   }
2491 
2492   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2493            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2494 
2495   // Destroy old participant context if present
2496   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2497     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2498     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2499   }
2500 
2501   // Create new participant context for the future host
2502   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2503       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2504       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2505       .enable_audio = true,
2506       .enable_video = true,
2507       .encryption_enabled = true,
2508   };
2509 
2510   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2511   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2512     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2513     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2514   }
2515 
2516   // Attempt connection to future host
2517   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2518   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2519     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2520     return pconn;
2521   }
2522 
2523   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2524 
2525   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2526   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2527   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2528   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2529 
2530   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2531            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2532 
2533   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2534   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2535 
2536   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2537   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2538 
2539   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2540 }

  discovery_config_t * config)


Create a new discovery session.


Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 52 of file

52                                                                                 {
53   if (!config) {
54     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
59   if (!session) {
60     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
61     return NULL;
62   }
63 
64   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
65   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
66   session->acds_transport = NULL;
67   session->peer_manager = NULL;
68   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
69   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
70   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
71   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
72   session->stun_servers = NULL;
73   session->stun_count = 0;
74   session->turn_servers = NULL;
75   session->turn_count = 0;
76 
77   // Initialize host liveness detection
78   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
80   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
81   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
82   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
83   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
84   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
85 
86   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
87            session->host_ctx);
88 
89   // Load or generate identity key
90   char identity_path[256];
91   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
92   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
93     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
94     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
95       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
96       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
97       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
99         // Save for future use
100         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
107     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
108     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
109   } else {
110     char fingerprint[65];
111     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
112     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
113   }
114 
115   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
116   const options_t *opts = options_get();
117   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
118     // Load all identity keys from options
119     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
120       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
121       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
122       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
123       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
124         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
125         char fingerprint[65];
126         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
127         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
128       } else {
129         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
130       }
131     }
132     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
133     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
134   } else {
135     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
136     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
137     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
138   }
139 
140   // ACDS connection info
141   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
143   }
144   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
145     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
146   } else {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
148   }
149   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
150 
151   // Session string (if joining)
152   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
153     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
154     session->is_initiator = false;
155   } else {
156     session->is_initiator = true;
157   }
158 
159   // Callbacks
160   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
161   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
162   session->on_error = config->on_error;
163   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
164   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
165   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
166 
167   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
168             session->acds_port);
169 
170   return session;
171 }

175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");

177   }
178 
179   // Close ACDS connection
180   if (session->acds_transport) {
181     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
182     session->acds_transport = NULL;
183   }
184   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
185     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
186     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
187   }
188 
189   // Destroy host context if active
190   if (session->host_ctx) {
191     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
192     session->host_ctx = NULL;
193   }
194 
195   // Destroy participant context if active
196   if (session->participant_ctx) {
197     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
198     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
202   if (session->peer_manager) {
203     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
204     session->peer_manager = NULL;
205   }
206 
207   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
208   if (session->stun_servers) {
209     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
210     session->stun_servers = NULL;
211     session->stun_count = 0;
212   }
213 
214   if (session->turn_servers) {
215     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
216     session->turn_servers = NULL;
217     session->turn_count = 0;
218   }
219 
220   SAFE_FREE(session);
221 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect
  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2371 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2371                                                                                                                      {
2372   if (!session) {
2373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2374     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2375   }
2376 
2377   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2378 
2379   // Initialize migration context
2380   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2381   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2382   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2383   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2384 
2385   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2386   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2387   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2388   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2389   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2390   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2391   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2392 
2393   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2394 
2395   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2396   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2397     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2398     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2399     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2400     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2401     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2402   }
2403 
2404   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2405   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2406   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2407 
2408   // Check if I am the future host
2409   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2410     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2411     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2412     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2413   } else {
2414     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2415     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2416              session->ring.future_host_port);
2417     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2418   }
2419 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1541 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1541                                                                                               {
1542   if (!session) {
1543     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1544   }
1545 
1546   // Handle state-specific processing
1547   switch (session->state) {
1548   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1549     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1550     // Note: don't use a hard timeout here - let the outer process timeout (snapshot delay) control max wait
1551     // The discovery_run() loop will keep calling this until session becomes active or process exits
1552     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1553     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1554 
1555     log_debug("★ WAITING_PEER: duration=%.3fs (no timeout, waiting for peer...)", wait_duration / 1e9);
1556 
1557     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1558       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1559       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1560       packet_type_t type;
1561       void *data = NULL;
1562       size_t len = 0;
1563       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1564 
1565       // Try to receive a packet
1566       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1567           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1568       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1569         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1570           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1571           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1572           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1573         } else {
1574           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1575           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1576           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1577         }
1578       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1579         // Timeout is normal, just return
1580       } else {
1581         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1582       }
1583     } else {
1584       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1585       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1586     }
1587     break;
1588   }
1589 
1590   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1591     // Handle NAT negotiation
1592     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1593       // Initialize negotiation
1594       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1595       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1596 
1597       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS so peers can evaluate us
1598       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1599       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1600         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1601       }
1602 
1603       // ACDS relays NETWORK_QUALITY only to OTHER participants, not back to sender
1604       // For now in discovery mode, assume we're the only one and become host immediately
1605       // A real implementation would wait for peer to join via PARTICIPANT_JOINED, then connect directly
1606       log_info("Proceeding as host (ACDS cannot relay peer quality in solo mode)");
1607       session->negotiate.our_quality.detection_complete = true;
1608       session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
1609       memset(&session->negotiate.peer_quality, 0, sizeof(session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1610       // Mark peer quality as detected but with worst NAT type so we win host election
1611       session->negotiate.peer_quality.detection_complete = true;
1612       session->negotiate.peer_quality.nat_type = ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC;
1613     }
1614 
1615     // Check for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS (in case we're waiting for host discovery)
1616     if (session->acds_transport && !session->is_initiator && !session->host_address[0]) {
1617       packet_type_t type;
1618       void *data = NULL;
1619       size_t len = 0;
1620       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1621 
1622       asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1623       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT) {
1624         acip_host_announcement_t *announcement = (acip_host_announcement_t *)data;
1625         log_info("Received HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS: %s:%u", announcement->host_address, announcement->host_port);
1626         SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, announcement->host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1627         session->host_port = announcement->host_port;
1628         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1629         // Continue to host role determination below
1630       } else if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1631         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1632       }
1633     }
1634 
1635     // Determine host role (now that peer_quality is set)
1636     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1637       log_info("Determining host role based on NAT qualities...");
1638       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1639       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1640         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1641 
1642         if (session->is_host) {
1643           // We become the host
1644           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1645                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1646                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1647           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1648           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1649           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1650 
1651           // Fallback to localhost if NAT detection failed to determine address
1652           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1653             log_warn("NAT address empty, using localhost fallback");
1654             SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->host_address));
1655           }
1656 
1657           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1658         } else {
1659           // They are host (shouldn't happen in solo mode, but handle it)
1660           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1661                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1662                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1663                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1664           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1665           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1666 
1667           // If host address is empty, wait for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS instead of using fallback
1668           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1669             log_info("Waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS to discover host address...");
1670             // Stay in NEGOTIATING state to continue waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT
1671             break;
1672           }
1673 
1674           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1675         }
1676       } else {
1677         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1678         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1679       }
1680     }
1681     break;
1682   }
1683 
1684   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1685     // Start hosting
1686     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1687       // Create host context with configured port
1688       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1689 
1690       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1691           .port = host_port,
1692           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1693           .max_clients = 32,
1694           .encryption_enabled = true,
1695       };
1696 
1697       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1698       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1699         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1700         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1701         break;
1702       }
1703 
1704       // Start listening
1705       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1706       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1707         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1708         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1709         break;
1710       }
1711 
1712       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1713     }
1714 
1715     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1716     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1717       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1718     }
1719 
1720     // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so participants can discover us
1721     int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1722     acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
1723     memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1724     memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->host_id, 16);
1725     SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
1726     announcement.host_port = host_port;
1727     announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
1728 
1729     if (session->acds_transport) {
1730       packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
1731                                 sizeof(announcement), 0);
1732       log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS at %s:%u", announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
1733     }
1734 
1735     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1736     break;
1737   }
1738 
1739   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1740     // Connect to host as participant
1741 
1742     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1743     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1744       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1745       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1746         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1747         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1748         break;
1749       }
1750 
1751       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1752       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1753         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1754                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1755 
1756         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1757         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1758             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1759 
1760         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1761           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1762           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1763           break;
1764         }
1765 
1766         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1767         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1768         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1769       }
1770 
1771       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1772       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1773         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1774         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1775 
1776         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1777           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1778                     timeout_ms);
1779 
1780           // Check if we have retries remaining
1781           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1782           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1783             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1784             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1785 
1786             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1787                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1788 
1789             // Wait for backoff period
1790             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1791 
1792             // Destroy old peer manager
1793             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1794             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1795 
1796             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1797             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1798             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1799               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1800               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1801 
1802               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1803               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1804                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1805                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1806                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1807               }
1808 
1809               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1810             }
1811 
1812             // Reset connection state for retry
1813             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1814             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1815 
1816             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1817                      max_attempts + 1);
1818           } else {
1819             // No more retries - connection failed
1820             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1821 
1822             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1823             char error_msg[256];
1824             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1825                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1826             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1827 
1828             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1829             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1830               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1831               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1832               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1833             }
1834 
1835             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1836           }
1837         }
1838       }
1839 
1840       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1841       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1842       if (session->acds_transport) {
1843         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1844         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1845         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1846         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1847 
1848         fd_set readfds;
1849         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1850         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1851 
1852         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1853         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1854           // Data available to read
1855           packet_type_t type;
1856           void *data = NULL;
1857           size_t len = 0;
1858           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1859 
1860           asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1861               packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1862 
1863           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1864             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1865             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1866             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1867           } else {
1868             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1869           }
1870         }
1871       }
1872 
1873       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1874       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1875       break;
1876     }
1877 
1878     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1879       // Create participant context for this session
1880       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1881           .address = session->host_address,
1882           .port = session->host_port,
1883           .enable_audio = true,
1884           .enable_video = true,
1885           .encryption_enabled = true,
1886       };
1887 
1888       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1889       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1890         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1891         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1892         break;
1893       }
1894 
1895       // Attempt connection
1896       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1897       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1898         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1899         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1900         break;
1901       }
1902 
1903       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1904 
1905       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1906       // Get the socket from participant context
1907       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1908       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1909         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1910         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1911         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1912         break;
1913       }
1914 
1915       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1916       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1917       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1918       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1919       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1920       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1921       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1922       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1923       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1924 
1925       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1926       if (result != 0) {
1927         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1928         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1929         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1930         break;
1931       }
1932 
1933       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1934       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1935       packet_type_t packet_type;
1936       void *payload = NULL;
1937       size_t payload_len = 0;
1938 
1939       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1940       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1941         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1942         if (payload) {
1943           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1944         }
1945         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1946         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1947         break;
1948       }
1949 
1950       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1951         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1952         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1953         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1954         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1955         break;
1956       }
1957 
1958       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1959       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1960       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1961 
1962       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1963         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1964         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1965         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1966         break;
1967       }
1968 
1969       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1970                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1971 
1972       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1973       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1974       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1975       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1976       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1977       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1978       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1979       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1980       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1981       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1982 
1983       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1984       if (result != 0) {
1985         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1986         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1987         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1988         break;
1989       }
1990 
1991       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1992       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1993     }
1994 
1995     // Check if connection is established
1996     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1997     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1998     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1999               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
2000 
2001     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
2002       // Log more details about why it's not connected
2003       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
2004     }
2005 
2006     if (is_connected) {
2007       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
2008       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2009     } else {
2010       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
2011                 "is_connected=%d)",
2012                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
2013     }
2014     break;
2015   }
2016 
2017   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
2018     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
2019     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
2020       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
2021       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
2022 
2023       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
2024 
2025       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
2026         // Data available to read
2027         packet_type_t type;
2028         void *data = NULL;
2029         size_t len = 0;
2030         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
2031 
2032         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
2033             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
2034 
2035         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2036           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
2037           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
2038           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
2039         }
2040       }
2041     }
2042 
2043     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
2044     if (!session->is_host) {
2045       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
2046       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
2047       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2048         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
2049         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
2050 
2051         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
2052         const options_t *opts = options_get();
2053         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
2054           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
2055           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2056           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2057           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2058         }
2059 
2060         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
2061         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
2062         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
2063       }
2064     }
2065     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2066     break;
2067 
2068   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
2069     // Host migration in progress
2070     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
2071     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
2072       // Migration completed successfully
2073       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2074       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
2075     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
2076       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
2077       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
2078       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2079       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2080     }
2081     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2082     break;
2083 
2084   default:
2085     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
2086     break;
2087   }
2088 
2089   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2090 }

    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_cipher,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_kex,

    session_participant_is_connected(),
    discovery_session_t::session_string,

    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_kex_algorithms,

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1344 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1344                                                                         {
1345   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1346 
1347   if (!session) {
1348     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1349   }
1350 
1351   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1352            session->session_string);
1353 
1354   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1355   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1356     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1357     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1358   }
1359 
1360   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1361   // Connect to ACDS
1362   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1363   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1364     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1365     return result;
1366   }
1367   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1368 
1369   // Check again after connection
1370   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1371     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1372     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1373   }
1374 
1375   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1376   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1377   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1378   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1379 
1380   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1381   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1382     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1383     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1384     return result;
1385   }
1386 
1387   // Store identity key in handshake context
1388   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1389   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1390   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1391   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1392 
1393   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1394   {
1395     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1396     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1397     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1398     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1399 
1400     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1401     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1402       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1403       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1404       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1405                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1406       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1407     }
1408 
1409     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1410       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1411                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1412       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1413       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1414       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1415     }
1416 
1417     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1418     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1419     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1420 
1421     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1422     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1423     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1424     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1425       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1426       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1427       return result;
1428     }
1429     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1430   }
1431 
1432   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1433   {
1434     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1435     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1436     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1437     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1438 
1439     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1440     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1441       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1442       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1443       return result;
1444     }
1445 
1446     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1447     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1448 
1449     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1450       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1451       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1452       return result;
1453     }
1454   }
1455 
1456   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1457   {
1458     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1459     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1460     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1461     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1462 
1463     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1464     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1465       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1466       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1467       return result;
1468     }
1469 
1470     result =
1471         crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1472     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1473 
1474     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1475       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1476       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1477       return result;
1478     }
1479   }
1480 
1481   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1482   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1483   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1484   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1485     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1486     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1487     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1488     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1489 
1490     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1491     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1492       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1493       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1494       return result;
1495     }
1496 
1497     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1498     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1499 
1500     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1501       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1502       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1503       return result;
1504     }
1505   } else {
1506     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1507   }
1508 
1509   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1510 
1511   // Check again after handshake
1512   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1513     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1514     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1515   }
1516 
1517   // Create or join session
1518   if (session->is_initiator) {
1519     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1520     result = create_session(session);
1521     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1522              session->participant_ctx, result);
1523 
1524     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1525       // Initiator also needs to register itself as a participant so it can receive PARTICIPANT_JOINED notifications
1526       // when other peers join. This makes the initiator discoverable to other participants.
1527       log_info("Initiator: now registering self as participant in created session");
1528       result = join_session(session);
1529       log_info("Initiator: join_session result=%d", result);
1530     }
1531     return result;
1532   } else {
1533     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1534     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1535     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1536              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1537     return result;
1538   }
1539 }

2281     // 1. Collect NETWORK_QUALITY from all connected participants (TODO: request fresh data)

Session Session context

2093   if (!session) {

2095     return;
2096   }
2097 
2098   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
2099 
2100   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
2101   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
2102     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
2103     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));

2105     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2106 
2107     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg),
2108                               0);
2109   }
2110
